{{Quote|We all have needs and... '''hungers'''. Your "soup" is a dark, complex stew, Jedi. I sense a kinship between us.|Sajé Tasha to [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]]|<ref name="Republic73">[[Republic 73|''Republic'' 73]]</ref>}}
'''Sajé Tasha''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Anzat (species)|Anzati]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] who served the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. She had long, [[Color/Legends|white]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and sported clawed [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] [[Cortosis gauntlet/Legends|gauntlets]] that had been given to her as gifts.

==Biography==
[[File:Saje2.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Tasha with her clawed [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis gauntlets]].]]
Approximately sixteen [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]], Tasha was hired by [[Sora Bulq/Legends|Sora Bulq]] and [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Viento]] to assassinate former [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Finis Valorum/Legends|Finis Valorum]]. Valorum boarded the [[AA-9 Coruscant freighter/Legends|AA-9 Coruscant freighter]] ''[[Star of Iskin]]'', and Tasha followed and killed him, drinking his "[[Soup (Anzati)|soup]]." Tasha planted a bomb on the ''Star'' and exited before the ship lifted off. The ''Star of Iskin'' was [[Terrorist attack of 14:9:19|destroyed]] to cover her tracks.

Tasha kept in contact with Bulq and his [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] clique after the Valorum job. About thirty-one months after [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]], two Anzati friends of hers were hired by Bulq to train [[Morgukai]] [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] on [[Saleucami/Legends|Saleucami]].

Around this same [[Time/Legends|time]], Sajé was on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and heard through the grape vine that [[Khaleen Hentz/Legends|Khaleen Hentz]] was looking to hire her. But as she always researched people, Tasha rightly suspected that [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]], Hentz's constant companion, was behind the request. Vos had come seeking information about the Valorum job and her connection with Senator Viento. Vos drew her into a heated confrontation to reveal what she knew, she promised to "relieve" him of the pain he caused and eventually he managed to draw the memories of her with his [[Psychometry/Legends|psychometric]] abilities, leading him to believe Sora Bulq was the "Second [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]," [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]]. Since he knew the identity of her other employer, Tasha prepared to make him "one of the [[Silent Voices]], forever". But before she could drink his soup, Khaleen struck her with a metal pole and aimed a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] at her. Tasha fled the scene, having also lost her gauntlets in the process. She vowed vengeance on Quinlan and Khaleen.

==Behind the scenes==
In the [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse]] release of [[Star Wars: Republic: Trackdown|''Trackdown'']], [[Dexter Jettster/Legends|Dexter Jettster]] calls her Sajé Tasha, but when she grabs Khaleen Hentz, she calls herself Saljé Tasha. The 2015 [[Marvel Comics|Marvel]] re-release of ''[[Star Wars Omnibus: Clone Wars Volume 3: The Republic Falls]]'' corrects all references of her to be Sajé.

Unlike other Anzati, Sajé does not possess enlarged nostrils the way other Anzati do. It's not known if this was accidental or deliberate on the part of the artist.

Of note, "[[The Lost One]]," an episode of the [[The Clone Wars: The Lost Missions|sixth season]] of the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' television series, would create a continuity error when it showed Finis Valorum—<ref name="Lost One">{{TCW|The Lost One}}</ref> Tasha's victim from ''Trackdown''—<ref name="Republic73" />alive in<ref name="Lost One" /> [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]],<ref name="FF-2014 60: Sifo-Dyas">{{FactFile2014|60|20 BBY 67-70|Sifo-Dyas' Legacy}}</ref> one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] after ''Trackdown'' depicted Tasha killing Valorum<ref name="Republic73" /> in [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> [[Lucasfilm]] never resolved the continuity error before declaring [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]] material [[Canon|non-canon]].<ref name="EU">{{SW|url=news/the-legendary-star-wars-expanded-universe-turns-a-new-page|text=The Legendary Star Wars Expanded Universe Turns a New Page}}</ref>
